pattern: Splitting a small array into exactly 3 contiguous subarrays is equivalent to picking the two smallest values among nums[1..n-1] as the starts of the second and third pieces.
hints: The cost of a division is nums[0] (always fixed) plus the first elements of the second and third subarrays.
The second and third subarrays start at indices i and j where 1 <= i < j <= n-1; you want to minimize nums[i] + nums[j].
With n at most 50, simply pick the two smallest values from nums[1:].

edge_inputs.description: minimum of suffix is at last position, don't skip it
greedy left-to-right would pick wrong pair

stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(3, 50)
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 50) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
